items.title: What does 99.9% uptime mean for inference?
items.description: Reliability numbers are easy to publish. We break down what 99%, 99.9%, and 99.99% uptime actually require, the failure domains each tier has to survive, and the questions to ask any inference provid…

items.title: Together AI brings Thinking Machines Lab’s new model Inkling on day 0
New in Together GPU Clusters: Reliability and control for production GPU clusters
items.description: See how Together AI is improving production GPU clusters with passive health checks, node repair, stronger Slurm reliability, OIDC, and startup scripts.

items.title: Open, convenient and predictable: Introducing Provisioned Throughput
items.description: Provisioned Throughput gives you reserved inference capacity for frontier open models like MiniMax M3 and GLM-5.2. Token-based pricing, a 99% uptime SLA, and up to 90% lower cost than proprietary API…

items.title: Announcing our $800M Series C to accelerate the shift to open-source AI
items.description: We raised $800M to accelerate the shift to open-source AI. Here's why the economics of closed models don't scale, and what we're building next.

items.title: Together AI at ICML 2026: frontier research across the full stack
items.description: Nine papers at ICML 2026 across the full stack. The research that becomes the Together platform. Find us at booth B714 in Seoul.

items.title: ParallelKernelBench: Frontier LLMs can't write fast multi-GPU kernels (yet)
items.description: ParallelKernelBench tests whether LLMs can write fast multi-GPU CUDA kernels across 87 real workloads. The best model solves under a third, but a few generated kernels beat any public implementation.

items.title: Kimi K2.7 Code vs Claude Fable 5: Landing pages that cost 94% less
items.description: We generated 12 landing pages with Kimi K2.7 Code and Claude Fable 5. Kimi cost 94% less and scored within a few points on every page. Here's what actually moved the needle.

items.title: Building trust in enterprise AI: Together AI earns ISO 27001:2022 certification
items.description: Together AI has earned ISO 27001:2022 certification, validating our commitment to enterprise-grade security for production AI workloads.

items.title: Serving MiniMax-M3 for efficient inference: Unlocking 1M-Token Context and Multimodality Without Regrets
items.description: How Together served MiniMax-M3 efficiently with KV-block-major sparse attention, paged MSA decode, optimized index scoring, and a Rust-based multimodal gateway.

items.title: How Together AI built the world’s fastest speech-to-text stack
items.description: Together AI built the fastest speech-to-text stack on Artificial Analysis by treating ASR as a full-path systems problem, not just a GPU inference problem.

items.title: Benchmarking inference at scale: coding agents
items.description: Real-world inference benchmarks for coding agents: 31% more TPS than TensorRT-LLM, 2× better TTFT at saturation, and 76% lower cost than Claude Opus 4.6.

items.title: Together AI and Pearl Research Labs Team Up to Reduce the Cost of AI Inference
items.description: Together AI partners with Pearl Research Labs to launch a discounted Pearl-powered inference endpoint for Gemma-4-31B-it-pearl, using Proof of Useful Work to turn AI workloads into crypto emissions.

items.title: Violin: An open-source video translation skill that breaks language barriers
items.description: Violin is an open-source AI video translation tool that combines speech recognition, LLM translation, and text-to-speech to make video content accessible across languages.

items.title: Introducing voice finder — a new tool to quickly find the right voice for your app from over 600+ voices
items.description: Voice finder helps developers search, match, filter, and audition 600+ voices across Together AI TTS models using natural-language prompts or uploaded audio samples.

items.title: Serving DeepSeek-V4: why million-token context is an inference systems problem
items.description: DeepSeek-V4 makes million-token context a serving-systems problem. Together AI explores the inference work behind V4 on NVIDIA HGX B200, including compressed KV layouts, prefix caching, kernel maturi…

items.title: Deploy and inference any model from HuggingFace
items.description: Learn how to deploy any Hugging Face model in one session using Goose and Together's Dedicated Container Inference. Skip the setup complexity — one prompt gets your model running in a production-grad…

items.title: Foundational research powering efficient inference at scale
items.description: As AI moves from research to production, the challenge for AI-native teams shifts from building models to running them — efficiently, reliably, and at scale.
